history.content:
<thought>
The agent is facing north. The cell directly ahead is a floor ('.'), which is safe. The goal is visible to the northeast, but lava cells block the direct eastward path from that cell. Moving forward is the first step toward navigating around the lava and reaching the goal.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The agent is facing north, and the cell directly ahead is a floor ('.'), which is safe. The goal is visible northeast, but the immediate east cell is lava. Moving forward will bring the agent closer to the goal while staying safe.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The cell directly ahead is a wall ('#'), so I cannot move forward. The goal 'G' is two cells to the east (right). I am facing north, so I need to turn right to face east. The cell to the east is a safe floor ('.'), so turning right is the correct move.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The goal G is visible two cells directly ahead. The cell immediately in front of me is a safe floor ('.'), and moving forward will bring me one step closer to the goal. There are no obstacles or lava in my intended path.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The goal G is directly in front of me. Moving forward will reach the goal safely, as the cell ahead is G, not lava or a wall. Therefore, I will move forward.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>
